hints.text: Plates have a blue strip left, but **short plates are very common** (unlike most of Europe)
Landscape = slightly hilly **dry agricultural** fields, 1–2 lane roads, cacti & dry shrubs (Mediterranean)
**Limestone rock walls** everywhere — this alone is usually enough to know you're in Malta
Low buildings, white-to-beige/limestone walls; traditional **covered balconies** (often brightly painted)
Street = **"triq"** on almost every street sign
**Double yellow no-parking lines** (like UK/Ireland); bus stops common (often naming the location)
**English** primary + **Maltese** (Latin w/ unique **Ċ Ġ Ħ Ż**); Gozo = stone barriers + bus routes all in the **300s**
